originalTitle: £123 for a child's England kit - have prices gone too far?
title: Big News! Is England's New Football Shirt Too Pricey?
content:
Hello, friends! It's your kid-friendly reporter here, bringing you exciting news from our amazing continent, Africa! We love football here, don't we? It's a game of joy and teamwork! Today, we are talking about football shirts. You know, the cool clothes our favourite players wear. There's a lot of chatter about the new football shirts for the England team. They are getting ready for a super big game called the World Cup! But grown-ups are asking a big question. Is the new England football kit too, too expensive? Let's find out together!

What is a Football Kit?

A football kit is a special uniform. It's what football players wear when they play. It has a shirt, shorts, and socks. Each team has its own special colours and badge. The badge is like a special picture on the shirt. It shows which country or team you play for. England's kit has a lion on it! It's their special animal. When you wear a kit, you feel like a real player. It makes you feel part of the team! **Did you know?** Football is the most popular sport in the whole wide world! So many people love to kick a ball!

What is the World Cup?

The World Cup is like the biggest football party ever! It happens only once every four years. Teams from all over the world come together. They play exciting matches to see who is the best. It's a super special event. Everyone watches and cheers for their favourite teams! The next World Cup will be in the year 2026. That's why England needs a new kit! Our African teams also try very hard to go to the World Cup! We cheer for them loudly!

England's New Kit for 2026

England is getting ready for the 2026 World Cup. They have a brand new kit. It is shiny and new! It has the special England colours, maybe white or red. The lion badge looks very proud on the shirt. It is a very smart kit. Many children in England, and all over the world, dream of wearing this kit. They want to feel like their football heroes when they kick the ball. **Did you know?** The World Cup trophy is made of real gold! It is very, very heavy!

Oh No! The Price Tag!

Now, here is the tricky part, my friends. This new England kit costs a lot of money. It costs about £123! That is a very big number for a child's shirt, shorts, and socks. Let me tell you what £123 could buy. It could buy many, many school books. Or it could buy many yummy meals for a family. Or even a new bicycle! It's a lot of money, more than what many families can easily spend.

Why Are Grow-Ups Talking About This?

Many grown-ups are worried about this high price. They think it is too much for families. Parents want their children to be happy. They want them to feel good wearing their team's kit. But if the kit costs so much, many children might not be able to get one. This makes parents sad. Even people who know a lot about money and clothes are saying it's too expensive. They call these people "experts." Experts think companies should make things more fair. **Did you know?** You don't need fancy shoes or clothes to play football! Many kids in Africa play amazing football with bare feet!

Who Makes These Kits? And Why So Pricey?

Big companies make these football kits. They have special machines and many workers. They design the shirts to look very cool. They use special cloth that helps players run fast. Making these kits costs money. The companies pay their workers and buy the cloth. But some people think they are making too much money. They charge too much for the kits. The World Cup makes everyone super excited. Maybe companies think people will pay anything!

What Can Kids Do If They Can't Get One?

It's okay if you can't get the new, super expensive kit. Football is still fun! You can wear an old kit if you have one. Or a simple t-shirt in your team's colours. You can even make your own special football shirt with crayons and an old t-shirt! The most important thing is to love the game. It's about playing with friends. It's about having fun and being healthy! That doesn't cost any money at all. **Did you know?** The first football kits looked very simple, like regular clothes! No fancy designs back then!

Cheaper Ways to Be a Fan!

Good news! Sometimes, companies make kits that are not so super expensive. They might make a "replica" kit. This means it looks almost the same as the fancy one. But it uses slightly different cloth, so it costs less money. It's still very cool! You can also wear a cap or a scarf with your team's colours. Those are not as expensive. Being a fan means cheering loudly and loving the game, not just having the newest shirt.

My Message from Africa

Here in Africa, we know the magic of football. We love to watch our national teams play! Many children here make their own footballs from old plastic bags or rags. They might not have fancy kits, but their hearts are full of football passion. It teaches us that the spirit of the game is more important than expensive clothes. Football connects us all, no matter where we are or what we wear! **Did you know?** Some of the best football players in the world come from Africa! They learned to play with simple balls and lots of passion!

Let's Keep Loving Football!

So, while the new England kit is very, very expensive, let's remember what truly matters. It's the joy of the game. It's the teamwork. It's the fun of kicking a ball. Whether you wear a brand new kit or just a simple t-shirt, you are a football fan! Let's hope that companies will think about kids and families next time. Football should be for everyone, always! Stay tuned for more fun news from Africa!
summary: The new England football kit for kids costs a lot of money, making grown-ups wonder if it's too expensive for families to buy, but playing football is still fun no matter what you wear.
whyMatters: This is important because everyone should be able to enjoy football, and expensive clothes should not stop children from feeling part of their favourite team or playing the game they love.

originalTitle: Women's game needs more respect with officiating - Bompastor
title: Women's game needs more respect with officiating - Bompastor
content: Chelsea manager Sonia Bompastor says the women's game needs "more respect" after her side have a goal controversially disallowed in their Women's Champions League quarter-final first leg against Arsenal.
